hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in Finance and Marketing (Dual): Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with a Finance and Marketing dual specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Direct and Indirect Taxes, Investment Analysis, Consumer Behaviour, Market Research, Indian Financial System, Risk Management and Behavioural Finance, Retail Marketing, and Digital Marketing in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA Finance and Marketing (Dual): NIRF #62, NAAC A++, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options), 4 scholarship categories. Finance subjects (Direct Taxes, Investment Analysis, Indian Financial System, Risk and Behavioural Finance) combined with Marketing subjects (Consumer Behaviour, Market Research, Retail Marketing, Digital Marketing) in one MBA. Unavailable as dual at Amity, NMIMS, or MUJ.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Finance and Marketing (Dual) is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), Bengaluru. JAIN holds NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #73 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The Finance and Marketing dual specialisation combines Sem 3-4 subjects from both domains in a single programme. Sem 3 covers Direct and Indirect Taxes, Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management, Consumer Behavior and Insights, and Market Research Tools alongside Business Research Methods and Operations Management core subjects. Sem 4 covers Indian Financial System, Risk Management and Behavioural Finance, Retail Marketing and Service Management, and Digital Marketing and Brand Management.

This dual specialisation is designed for professionals who manage revenue-facing roles requiring both financial acumen and marketing skills: brand managers who own P&L, marketing finance controllers, retail general managers, or professionals in banking marketing, fintech product marketing, and FMCG brand finance. JAIN offers this dual credential at the same fee range as single-spec MBAs at peer universities - a clear value differentiator.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Apply quantitative research methods to both market research studies and financial data analysis projects.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply process design and efficiency frameworks as a complement to both marketing operations and financial management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Direct and Indirect Taxes
sem3Subjects.description: Apply corporate income tax, GST, and TDS compliance to financial management and P&L ownership in marketing finance roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply DCF, relative valuation, and portfolio theory to corporate capital allocation and financial decision-making.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse buying behaviour, cultural influences, and decision journeys to build marketing strategy and brand positioning.

sem3Subjects.name: Market Research Tools
sem3Subjects.description: Apply SPSS, survey design, and secondary data analysis to generate market intelligence for integrated finance and marketing decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop integrated finance and marketing strategy, evaluate competitive positioning, and design dual-domain business growth plans.

sem4Subjects.name: Indian Financial System and Financial Markets
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse capital market instruments, RBI policy, SEBI regulations, and financial market structure for cross-functional finance roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Risk Management and Behavioural Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Apply behavioural economics to consumer and investor decision-making, and manage financial risk in marketing-led business contexts.

sem4Subjects.name: Retail Marketing and Service Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage category strategy, service quality design, and omnichannel execution for general managers in retail and financial services.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Marketing and Brand Management
sem4Subjects.description: Build digital brand equity, manage performance marketing campaigns, and integrate financial metrics into digital marketing ROI analysis.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
Finance and Marketing dual MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FMCG brand finance, Banking marketing and product, Retail and e-commerce, Financial services marketing, Fintech product management, Consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Marketing Finance Analyst, Brand Finance Analyst, Retail Merchandising Analyst, Banking Product Analyst, Fintech Marketing Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Finance Manager, Marketing P&L Manager, Retail General Manager, Banking Product Manager, FMCG Marketing Finance Lead

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CMO with P&L, VP Marketing Finance, Retail Business Head, Chief Revenue Officer, Head of Marketing Finance

**Top hiring organisations:** Unilever India, P&G India, ITC, Nestle India, HDFC Bank Marketing, Axis Bank Product, Kotak Marketing, Flipkart Marketing Finance, Amazon India, Deloitte Marketing Advisory, KPMG Finance Advisory

skills.body:
The JAIN Finance and Marketing dual specialisation builds integrated skills for P&L management, brand finance, and revenue-facing roles:

**Finance skills:**
- Direct and Indirect Taxes: corporate tax, GST, and TDS compliance for P&L owners managing marketing budgets and brand finance
- Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management: DCF and relative valuation for capital allocation and marketing investment decisions
- Risk Management and Behavioural Finance: financial risk management combined with behavioural consumer insight for integrated brand and finance decisions

**Marketing skills:**
- Consumer Behaviour and Insights: customer journey analysis and buying behaviour understanding for brand positioning and marketing strategy
- Market Research Tools: SPSS and survey analytics for market intelligence generation supporting both marketing and financial decisions
- Digital Marketing and Brand Management: digital brand equity building, performance marketing management, and marketing ROI integration

**Cross-functional skills:**
- Retail Marketing and Service Management: category strategy, omnichannel execution, and service quality management for general managers bridging finance and marketing
- Indian Financial System: capital markets, SEBI regulations, and financial products knowledge for marketing professionals in BFSI and fintech

**Soft skills:** Presenting integrated finance and marketing performance to business leadership, managing cross-functional P&L teams, building investment cases for marketing budgets, and communicating brand equity value to CFOs.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA Finance and Marketing (Dual) against peers:

**vs single-spec Finance or Marketing at Amity/NMIMS/MUJ:** Amity, NMIMS, and MUJ do not offer Finance and Marketing as a dual specialisation at comparable fees. Students at these universities must choose one domain. JAIN Finance and Marketing dual gives a combined Finance and Marketing credential in a single MBA programme - a differentiator for P&L management, brand finance, and integrated business roles.

**vs JAIN Finance (single spec) within the same university:** JAIN Finance covers Investment Analysis, Banking, Fintech, Wealth Management, and Risk Management in dedicated depth. JAIN Finance and Marketing dual replaces some finance depth (Fintech, Wealth Management) with marketing subjects (Consumer Behaviour, Retail Marketing, Digital Marketing). Students who need deep finance specialisation for banking or investment roles should choose single-spec Finance. Students managing revenue-facing roles combining finance and marketing should choose the dual.

**vs JAIN Marketing (single spec) within the same university:** JAIN Marketing covers B2B and B2C marketing, international marketing, brand management, and digital marketing in depth. JAIN Finance and Marketing dual replaces some marketing depth with finance subjects. Students targeting pure marketing management roles should choose single-spec Marketing. Students managing P&L or brand finance roles should choose the dual.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in Finance and Marketing (Dual) is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. Both Finance and Marketing specialisations are noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The dual specialisation certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA Finance and Marketing alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Aditya Srinivas
items.liked: Investment Analysis combined with Consumer Behaviour and Market Research gave me the dual credential my FMCG brand finance role requires. Digital Marketing and Brand Management subject was immediately applicable.
items.disliked: The breadth means neither Finance nor Marketing is at single-spec depth. Know this trade-off before choosing dual over single-spec.
items.body: I had 5 years in FMCG marketing and needed financial credibility for a brand finance transition. The JAIN Finance and Marketing dual MBA gave me exactly that. No other university at this fee offered both specialisations in one programme.

items.name: Kavya Iyer
items.liked: Dual specialisation gave me credibility in both brand and finance conversations. Risk Management and Behavioural Finance subject was a unique cross-domain combination.
items.disliked: Students hoping for deep finance technical skills like derivatives or wealth management should choose single-spec Finance. Dual is for breadth.
items.body: Good programme for revenue-facing management roles at South India FMCG and retail companies. Chennai companies valued the dual credential for cross-functional management positions.

items.name: Praveen Kumar
items.liked: The dual credential was a clear differentiator on my resume for roles requiring both P&L and brand management.
items.disliked: Some finance subjects like Direct Taxes felt basic compared to single-spec Finance depth.
items.body: Good value at the fee. Best suited for roles requiring cross-functional P&L and brand management. Not suitable if deep finance or deep marketing specialisation is the career goal.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the Finance and Marketing dual specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Is Finance and Marketing dual MBA better than single-spec Finance or Marketing?
faqs.answer: Dual specialisation provides breadth across both Finance and Marketing domains but at less depth than single-spec programmes. For P&L management, brand finance, FMCG marketing finance, and cross-functional general management roles, dual gives better career positioning. For deep banking, investment, or pure marketing specialisation, single-spec programmes at JAIN or peers offer stronger domain focus.

faqs.question: Does JAIN Finance and Marketing dual spec appear on the degree certificate?
faqs.answer: Yes. Both Finance and Marketing specialisations are noted on the marksheet and transcript. The dual specialisation credential is recognised for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission.

faqs.question: Which other dual specialisations does JAIN offer?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 7+ dual specialisation options including Marketing and Business Analytics, HR and Business Analytics, Marketing and HRM, HRM and Finance, and Finance and Business Analytics. These combinations give career flexibility not available at peer online MBA universities at comparable fees.

chooseIf: You want Finance and Marketing dual credentials from a NAAC A++ university in one programme at no additional cost over single-spec fees.
You are targeting brand finance, P&L management, FMCG marketing finance, or retail general management roles.
You want 3 flexible payment options and 4 scholarship categories at fees of Rs 1.75L-1.96L.
consider: If deep finance specialisation for banking, investment, or corporate treasury is the goal, JAIN's single-spec Finance or International Finance (ACCA) offers more domain depth.
If deep marketing specialisation for brand management or digital marketing careers is the goal, JAIN's single-spec Marketing or Digital Marketing and E-Commerce is more focused.
If data analytics combined with marketing is the goal, JAIN's Marketing and Business Analytics dual spec is a stronger option.
